body: This PR upgrades to use Julia 1.8
title: Upgrade to julia 1.8

body: This PR adds an up-deps script for easing the update of the checked-in manifest files. This script can be run with `julia .dev/up_deps.jl`
title: Add and apply up_deps script

body:
# PULL REQUEST

## Purpose and Content
This PR adds support for `DiffEqBase.add_tstop!` and `DiffEqBase.reinit!` to ClimaTimeSteppers (see their documentation [here](https://diffeq.sciml.ai/stable/basics/integrator/)). More generally, it matches the saving behavior of OrdinaryDiffEq as closely as possible, and it includes a detailed set of unit tests to verify this.

Here is a list of the changes in this PR:
- Allow users to specify `tstops` and `saveat` to set up an integrator. These are converted into min/max heaps (depending on the direction of integration) and stored in `integrator.tstops` and `integrator.saveat`. The original user-specified values are stored in `integrator._tstops` and `integrator._saveat`; these are used to obtain default arguments for `reinit!`.
- Remove `integrator.tdir`, since the direction of integration is encoded in the min/max heaps.
- Remove `integrator.prob`, since that value is stored in `integrator.sol.prob` (this counts for most of the changed files).
- Replace `integrator.adjustfinal` with `integrator.dtchangeable`, since the corresponding behavior should apply to all `tstops` (instead of only the final one), and since OrdinaryDiffEq's integrator also has a `dtchangeable` field (though this field is for internal use only). Change the default for this value from `false` to `true`.
- Change the default saving behavior to save at the initial and final times, and ensure that the final time is saved when `saveat isa Number`.
- Add method for `reinit!`, which works just as OrdinaryDiffEq's, except that it doesn't support `reinit_cache`, since reallocating our integrator's cache would serve no purpose (it also does not support `d_discontinuities` or `reset_dt`, since we do not have the corresponding features).
- Add methods for `add_tstop!` and `add_saveat!`, which work just as OrdinaryDiffEq's.
- Rename `adjust_dt!` to `set_dt!`.
- Fix type instability by ensuring that every integrator field has a concrete type.
- Fix floating point precision issues by rounding `integrator.t` to the nearest `tstop` if the two values are sufficiently close, just like OrdinaryDiffEq does.

## Benefits and Risks
This PR will allow ClimaCoupler to use ClimaTimeSteppers, and it will facilitate comparisons to OrdinaryDiffEq by giving us similar saving behavior.

## Linked Issues
- Closes #65 

## PR Checklist
- [x] This PR has a corresponding issue OR is linked to an SDI.
- [x] I have followed CliMA's codebase [contribution](https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/) and [style](https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/) guidelines OR N/A.
- [x] I have followed CliMA's [documentation policy](https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy).
- [x] I have checked all issues and PRs and I certify that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.
- [x] Unit tests are included OR N/A.
- [x] Code used in an integration test OR N/A.
- [x] All tests ran successfully on my local machine OR N/A.
- [x] All classes, modules, and function contain appropriate docstrings OR N/A.
- [x] Documentation has been added/updated OR N/A.
title: Add tstops/reinit and match saving behavior to OrdinaryDiffEq
